core.label: Go Fundamentals
core.description: Installation, syntax, types, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Installation & Go Toolchain
topics.description: Install Go, set up GOPATH, and use go run, go build, go mod. The Go toolchain is batteries-included.

topics.label: Hello World & Package Structure
topics.description: Understand package main, func main, and Go's file/package organization. Every Go program starts in package main.

topics.label: Variables & Types
topics.description: int, float64, string, bool, and short declaration (:=). Go is statically typed with type inference.

topics.label: Constants & iota
topics.description: Declare constants with const and use iota for auto-incrementing enums. Go has no enum keyword.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else (with init statement), switch, for (the only loop), break, continue. Go's for replaces while too.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Multiple return values, named returns, and variadic functions. Multiple returns are idiomatic for error handling.

topics.label: Pointers
topics.description: Pointer syntax with & and *. Go has pointers but no pointer arithmetic — safe by design.

topics.label: Strings, Runes & Bytes
topics.description: Strings are byte slices, runes represent Unicode code points. Understand the difference for text processing.

topics.label: Arrays & Slices
topics.description: Fixed-size arrays vs dynamic slices. Slices are Go's primary sequence type with append and slice expressions.

topics.label: Maps
topics.description: Key-value hash maps with make(map[K]V). Maps are the built-in dictionary type.

core.label: Structs, Interfaces & Methods
core.description: Go's type system, methods, and interface-based polymorphism.

topics.label: Structs
topics.description: Define composite types with struct. Structs are Go's primary data modeling tool — there are no classes.

topics.label: Methods
topics.description: Attach functions to types with receiver syntax. Methods on structs are Go's equivalent of class methods.

topics.label: Pointer vs Value Receivers
topics.description: Choose between pointer and value receivers based on mutation and size. Pointer receivers modify the original.

topics.label: Interfaces
topics.description: Implicit interface satisfaction — no implements keyword. Interfaces are Go's mechanism for polymorphism.

topics.label: Empty Interface & Type Assertions
topics.description: interface{} (or any) holds any value. Type assertions and type switches recover the concrete type.

topics.label: Embedding
topics.description: Compose types by embedding structs and interfaces. Embedding provides delegation without inheritance.

topics.label: Generics
topics.description: Type parameters with [T any] and constraints (Go 1.18+). Generics reduce code duplication for type-safe functions.

topics.label: Stringer & Common Interfaces
topics.description: Implement fmt.Stringer, io.Reader, io.Writer, error. These interfaces are used throughout the standard library.

core.label: Error Handling & I/O
core.description: Go's error pattern, file operations, and standard library I/O.

topics.label: Error Handling Pattern
topics.description: Return errors as values with if err != nil. Go's explicit error handling replaces exceptions.

topics.label: Custom Errors
topics.description: Create error types implementing the error interface. errors.Is and errors.As for error inspection.

topics.label: Wrapping Errors
topics.description: Wrap errors with fmt.Errorf(%w) for context. Error wrapping creates error chains for debugging.

topics.label: Defer, Panic & Recover
topics.description: defer for cleanup, panic for unrecoverable errors, recover to catch panics. Defer is used for file/lock cleanup.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with os and io packages. os.ReadFile and os.WriteFile for simple cases.

topics.label: io.Reader & io.Writer
topics.description: The fundamental I/O interfaces. Everything in Go (files, network, buffers) implements Reader/Writer.

topics.label: JSON Encoding/Decoding
topics.description: Marshal and unmarshal JSON with encoding/json. Struct tags control JSON field names.

topics.label: Working with HTTP
topics.description: Make HTTP requests with net/http client and parse responses. The standard library HTTP client is production-ready.

topics.label: Goroutines
topics.description: Launch concurrent functions with the go keyword. Goroutines are lightweight — you can run millions.

topics.label: Channels
topics.description: Communicate between goroutines with typed channels. Channels are Go's primary synchronization mechanism.

topics.label: Buffered Channels
topics.description: Channels with capacity for async sends. Buffered channels decouple sender and receiver timing.

topics.label: Select
topics.description: Multiplex channel operations with select. Select blocks until one case is ready.

topics.label: WaitGroups
topics.description: Wait for a collection of goroutines to finish with sync.WaitGroup.

topics.label: Mutexes
topics.description: Protect shared state with sync.Mutex and sync.RWMutex. Use mutexes when channels aren't the right fit.

topics.label: Context
topics.description: Propagate cancellation and deadlines with context.Context. Context is used in every Go server and API client.

topics.label: Concurrency Patterns
topics.description: Fan-out/fan-in, worker pools, pipelines, and rate limiting. Patterns for structuring concurrent programs.

topics.label: Race Detector
topics.description: Detect data races with go run -race. The race detector finds bugs that are impossible to catch manually.

core.label: Packages & Modules
core.description: Go modules, dependency management, and project organization.

topics.label: Go Modules
topics.description: Initialize with go mod init, manage dependencies with go.mod and go.sum. Modules are Go's dependency system.

topics.label: Package Organization
topics.description: Structure projects with internal/, cmd/, pkg/ conventions. Good package design makes Go projects maintainable.

topics.label: Exported vs Unexported
topics.description: Capital letter = exported, lowercase = unexported. Go's visibility is controlled by naming convention.

topics.label: go install & go generate
topics.description: Install binaries and run code generation. go generate automates code generation workflows.

topics.label: Vendoring
topics.description: Include dependencies in the vendor/ directory for reproducible builds.

topics.label: Build Tags & Cross-Compilation
topics.description: Conditional compilation with //go:build tags and cross-compile with GOOS/GOARCH.

topics.label: Standard Library Deep Dive
topics.description: Explore net/http, os, fmt, strings, strconv, sort, sync, time, and regexp.

topics.label: Go Doc & Documentation
topics.description: Write doc comments and use godoc. Documentation is generated from comments above declarations.

core.label: Testing
core.description: Go's built-in testing framework, benchmarks, and best practices.

topics.label: Unit Testing
topics.description: Write tests in _test.go files with func TestXxx(t *testing.T). Go's testing is built into the toolchain.

topics.label: Table-Driven Tests
topics.description: Parameterize tests with slices of test cases. The idiomatic Go pattern for comprehensive test coverage.

topics.label: Subtests
topics.description: Run named subtests with t.Run for better organization and selective execution.

topics.label: Benchmarks
topics.description: Measure performance with func BenchmarkXxx(b *testing.B). Benchmark before optimizing.

topics.label: Mocking & Interfaces
topics.description: Mock dependencies by defining interfaces and providing test implementations. Go's interfaces make mocking natural.

topics.label: Test Coverage
topics.description: Generate coverage reports with go test -cover. Visualize coverage with go tool cover.

topics.label: Fuzzing
topics.description: Find edge cases with Go's built-in fuzzing (Go 1.18+). Fuzzing tests with random inputs.

topics.label: go vet & staticcheck
topics.description: Static analysis with go vet and staticcheck. Catch bugs the compiler misses.

core.label: Web & Production Go
core.description: HTTP servers, databases, and production deployment.

topics.label: HTTP Server
topics.description: Build HTTP servers with net/http, handlers, and the new ServeMux (Go 1.22+). No framework needed for basics.

topics.label: Middleware Pattern
topics.description: Chain handlers for logging, auth, CORS, etc. Middleware is a function that wraps an http.Handler.

topics.label: Database Access (database/sql)
topics.description: Connect to databases with database/sql and drivers. The standard SQL interface works with any database.

topics.label: Configuration & Environment
topics.description: Read env vars, flags, and config files. os.Getenv and flag package for CLI applications.

topics.label: Logging (slog)
topics.description: Structured logging with the slog package (Go 1.21+). Structured logs are essential for production observability.

topics.label: Graceful Shutdown
topics.description: Handle OS signals and drain connections before exiting. Essential for zero-downtime deployments.

topics.label: Docker & Deployment
topics.description: Build minimal Docker images with multi-stage builds. Go produces static binaries ideal for containers.

topics.label: Profiling (pprof)
topics.description: Profile CPU, memory, and goroutines with pprof. Go has built-in profiling tools.
